name: Stone Returns
description: Brec has documented something that requires Stone in person. The Lazarus subjects are interfacing with pre-Grid storage crystals installed below — and Stone needs to see the readings herself.
accept_text: He has been preparing for this conversation for three months without knowing he was preparing for it. He puts both data folders on the monitoring station — the eight-month resonance shift documentation and the secondary shift he noticed after the sub-zero deliveries. 'I need her to see this in person,' he says. 'The secondary shift data — the four oldest subjects responding to the storage crystal frequency — I can describe it, but the instrument readings have a pattern that requires her eye to interpret correctly. I don't have her twenty years of experience reading crystal resonance.' He pauses. 'And there's something else I noticed this morning.' He opens the monitoring interface. 'The oldest subject — the one with the most complete crystal integration, no organic tissue, entirely crystal structure — its resonance frequency has been moving toward a specific target frequency for three months.' He looks at the display. 'Today it arrived. The frequency it's been moving toward — it's the same as what I'd expect from a storage medium that had been triggered to release.' He meets your eyes. 'It's trying to share something. Whatever is stored in the sub-zero crystals has established a resonance handshake with the oldest subject, and the oldest subject is now broadcasting on the storage frequency.' He looks at the display. 'I don't know how to read what it's broadcasting. Stone will.' He pauses. 'Tell her: the compound is doing what she designed it to do, but the signal it's responding to is three hundred years old. Tell her it's urgent.'
complete_text: Stone comes through the maintenance route — Brec's level-four junction — and doesn't stop at the door. She goes directly to the monitoring station and reads both folders without saying anything. Then she reads the secondary shift data again. Then she looks at the oldest subject through the observation window for a long moment. 'The handshake,' she says. Not to Brec, not to you. To herself. The specific tone of someone who has been working toward something for twenty years and has just seen the last piece from an unexpected direction. She goes to the interface and adjusts the display settings with the authority of someone who designed the system. The frequency pattern that was difficult to read becomes clear. She reads it. 'This is not a random signal.' She says it very quietly. 'The storage medium is transmitting a structured sequence. Compressed. Encoded.' She looks at Brec. 'How long has it been transmitting?' He tells her: since this morning, building for three months. She nods, already doing the calculation. 'Three hundred years in storage, eight months of gradual reorientation when the Grid reactivated, three months of building handshake, and now transmission.' She is very quiet. 'There is someone in there.' She says it the way someone says something they've been trying not to think and have just run out of the ability to not think it. 'Not a recording. Not data. A consciousness that has been stored in a pre-Grid crystal for three hundred years has found the only receiver within range — the oldest Lazarus subject — and is talking to us.' She looks at the display. 'I need to know what they're saying. And I need to know if they can hear us back.'
